summary:
A round bur is commonly used for precise bone removal during periradicular surgery because it offers better control and reduces damage to nearby tissues; other tools like fissure burs serve different purposes not related to direct bone reduction in these surgical settings. ```plaintext Summary: Round burs are preferred for removing bone during periradicular surgery as they offer high precision and minimize tissue trauma compared to other instruments which have distinct uses unsuitable for primary bone reduction tasks.
```
